_doc: Deployment profiles by RAM budget. Each profile picks the STRONGEST model that fits the tier (Q4_K_M) plus how much technique we can afford on top (more headroom = deeper ensemble/retrieval). The operations layer detects available RAM and selects a profile; every value maps to a real bench flag so a profile is directly runnable.
_quant: sizes are Q4_K_M approximate footprint (model + KV + nomic-embed ~0.4GB + OS headroom)

8gb.label: edge / lite (CPU or 8GB iGPU)
techniques: gate (skip retrieval when confident — saves the most on a small CPU model)
shallow capped retrieval only when needed
8gb.rationale: The model is the bottleneck and CPU-bound; we CANNOT afford the 5-arm council or K=5 self-consistency. Lean: a strong 4B + adaptive gate + shallow retrieval. Latency over depth.

16gb.label: standard (laptop / single T4)
16gb.rationale: The sweet spot. A strong 14B has headroom for the full council + Medprompt + hybrid retrieval. This is the main stack.

24gb.label: max (high-end / single L4)
24gb.rationale: The full stack. A 27-32B base + every technique + deep retrieval. Reasoning models (QwQ/R1-32B) for math-heavy workloads; qwen3.6-27B as the strong general default.

_selection: detect RAM → pick the largest tier that fits with ~20% headroom; reasoning workloads (math/proof) prefer the *_alts reasoning model at the tier; knowledge workloads prefer the general default.
